commit.message:
fix: align NER gap/zero-entity status gates across meta_json, semaphore, and flags

All three status determination paths now consistently gate NER gap FAIL
and zero-entity WARN behind doc_type != "unknown". Benchmark eval runs
(where document_metadata is intentionally disabled) no longer get false
FAIL/WARN verdicts for expected missing entities. Production runs with
classification enabled still get proper FAIL/WARN when extraction fails.

Add FilterLookup[str] deprecation check for NetBox 4.5.4+

NetBox 4.5.4 upgrades strawberry-django, causing deprecation warnings
when plugins use FilterLookup[str] in graphql.py. Only FilterLookup[str]
is affected — FilterLookup[int], FilterLookup[bool], etc. are fine.

The fix is to import StrFilterLookup from strawberry_django and use
StrFilterLookup[str] instead. For backwards compatibility with
NetBox < 4.5.4, use a try/except import pattern.

Fix OpenRouter page limit, add real-time extraction progress, bump workers

- Fix pdf_to_images_base64 max_pages from 10 to 100 (was causing missed
  peripheral blocks when using OpenRouter since only first 10 pages were
  sent as images)
- Add real-time progress tracking for extraction jobs (30-90% scales
  with per-PDF completion instead of staying stuck at 30%)
- Force line-buffered stdout in extraction script for live logging
- Increase max_workers from 7 to 90 for faster extraction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rewrite mutex as inline queue to guarantee operation serialization

Old acquire/release pattern had a microtask gap between lock acquisition
and work execution. New pattern runs the entire operation INSIDE .then(),
making concurrent execution physically impossible. Added sequence numbers
and logger.info (always visible, even in production) for debugging.

commit.message:
feat(framebuffer): truecolor + adaptive threshold for Braille rendering

Two improvements to Braille color rendering:
- Adaptive local luma threshold per cell (average luma of the 2x4 block)
  instead of a fixed 128 cutoff, so every cell contributes dots relative
  to its own content rather than a global brightness level.
- 24-bit truecolor (ESC[38;2;R;G;Bm) for the dot color instead of ANSI
  256, averaged only from pixels that set dots so the color reflects the
  lit area rather than the dark background.

commit.message:
fix(bingo): defer balance credit to end of draw animation

Previously _syncRoundFromContract() credited users.balance when the
RoundResolved event fired (during 'drawing' status), causing the frontend
to show the increased balance before the ball animation finished.

Changes:
- bingoEventService: skip balance credit when round stays in 'drawing'
  (RETURNING status guard). Credit only when transitioning directly to
  'resolved' (edge case: round skipped drawing state).
- bingoService.finalizeDrawing(): upgraded to atomically mark
  drawing→resolved AND credit winners from bingo_cards in one transaction.
  RETURNING guard prevents double-credit on concurrent calls.
- bingoSchedulerOnChain: use finalizeDrawing() in both roomLoop step 6
  and recoverDrawingRounds() instead of bare pool.query UPDATE.
- bump bolcoin-frontend → 8685dd0 (actual prize amounts in stats bar)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Validate sender key tweak proofs during InitiateTransfer (#5589)

## Summary

Adds sender key tweak proof verification to `InitiateTransfer` and
`InitiateTransferV2` on non-coordinator SOs. Each SO now compares the
coordinator's plaintext `SenderKeyTweakProofs` against the proofs it
independently decrypted from the transfer package, catching mismatches
before the transfer is persisted rather than only during the later
gossip-based `CommitSenderKeyTweaks` phase.

Follows up on #5554, which started sending `SenderKeyTweakProofs` in
`InitiateTransferRequest` but did not yet consume them on the receiving
side.

#### Other changes
- Added `verifySenderKeyTweakProofsMatch` helper in
`base_transfer_handler.go` for reuse across both handlers
- Added clarifying comments to distinguish
`verifySenderKeyTweakProofsMatch` (pre-persist, in-memory maps) from
`validateKeyTweakProofs` (post-persist, DB-backed)
- `InitiateTransfer` and `InitiateTransferV2` now error if
`ValidateTransferPackage` returns a nil key tweak map

## Test Plan

- 3 new unit tests for `verifySenderKeyTweakProofsMatch`: matching
proofs, missing proof, and mismatched proofs — all passing
- Full `go test ./so/handler/` passes (41s, 0 failures)
- `golangci-lint run ./so/handler/` — 0 issues

Resolves SPARK-472

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add 'custom' project type to /new-project skill

This commit adds a new "custom" project type option that creates a minimal
blank canvas project structure, giving users complete freedom to implement
their own project layout while maintaining the docs/ tracking structure.

Changes:

Skill Documentation:
- Update new-project.md: Add "Custom" to project type options with description
- Update new-project-impl.md: Add implementation details for custom type,
  specifying what to include and what to exclude

Bootstrap Script (scripts/new-project.sh):
- Update usage text to include 'custom' type
- Update validation regex to accept 'custom' as valid project type
- Add complete 'custom' case implementation that creates:
  - Minimal README.md with basic project structure
  - Minimal CLAUDE.md with guidance for custom projects
  - docs/ directory copied from template (planning, implementation, issues, changelog)
  - All placeholders replaced with actual project name
- Exclude from custom projects:
  - .mise.toml (user provides own tooling)
  - pyproject.toml/package.json (user defines dependencies)
  - Docker files (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, Makefile, nginx.conf)
  - .env.example (user creates if needed)
  - Source directories (src/, tests/)
- Update "Next steps" message to guide custom project users

Custom Project Structure:
```
projects/my-experiment/
├── README.md           # Minimal project info
├── CLAUDE.md          # Minimal guidance
└── docs/              # Planning and tracking
    ├── planning.md
    ├── implementation.md
    ├── issues.md
    └── changelog.md
```

Use Case: For users who want to experiment with their own project structure
while maintaining the documentation and tracking benefits of the workbench.

Testing: Verified with `./scripts/new-project.sh test-custom custom` which
creates minimal structure with only docs/ and basic markdown files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

bwrap/firejail backends.

commit.message:
feat(guardian): GitHub App webhook receiver with signature verification

Implements PR 3 of the BUILDPLAN — GitHub App integration that captures
webhook events into the raw_events pipeline. Includes HMAC SHA-256
signature verification with timing-safe comparison, content extraction
for 5 event types (issues, issue_comment, pull_request, pull_request_review,
push), contributor profile upsert on each event, and idempotency via
delivery ID uniqueness constraint.

25 tests covering unit (pure functions) and integration (endpoint behavior
with mocked DB queries). Sacred Four passes clean.

fix: remove duplicate data-reveal from elements with dedicated GSAP animations

Elements with both data-reveal and their own gsap.from() animation had a
conflict: the second from() recorded opacity=0 (already set by the first),
causing them to animate from 0 to 0 and stay invisible.

Affected: concept pillars, stats reviews, infos cards, infos map.

commit.message:
Theory audit: fix false Theorem 2, tighten Theorem 3, correct NML terminology

Theorem 2 (monotonicity) is false as stated: same shift + period multiple
does NOT guarantee partition refinement. Counterexample: D=4, p1=1, s=1,
p2=2, s=1 on checkerboard gives d*(2,1)=4 > 0=d*(1,1). Correct condition
requires velocity-matched shifts: s2 = m*s1 mod D.

Theorem 3 proof gap: the Omega(T) runs assumption does not imply per-site
RL rate convergence. Recommend restating for Bernoulli NML score (which
aligns with the code's actual selection criterion).

Code patches:
- Fix "exact NML" → "asymptotic Bernoulli NML" in all docstrings
- Fix template_bits_nml docstring: "BIC-type" not "NML"
- Update dataclass comments in repair.py and repair_nd.py

New deliverables:
- CLAIM_LEDGER.md: every claim classified with status
- THEORY_NOTE.md: corrected theorem suite with proofs
- VERDICT.md: assessment of current vs corrected manuscript
- REVISION_PLAN.md: prioritized fix list
- MANUSCRIPT_PATCH.md: concrete replacement text
- tests/test_theory.py: 22 tests (all pass) covering projection
  theorem, refinement monotonicity, counterexamples, nonidentifiability,
  RL separation, and score asymptotics
- scripts/find_counterexamples.py: finds 4628 1D + 66 2D counterexamples
  to false Theorem 2; 0 violations of corrected version
- scripts/compare_scores.py: MDL vs NML vs combinatorial comparison

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Sealed Transforms paper — premise is fundamentally flawed

WASM bytecode cannot hide embedded keys from a server operator,
making the "sealed wrapper" E2EE claim invalid without TEE hardware.

commit.message:
Show job run datetime instead of duration on overview (#91)

* Show job run datetime instead of duration on overview page

Users scanning the overview page care more about when a job ran than how long it took. Replace "Last run 5s" with "Last run Mar 10, 2:32 PM" style formatting using a new formatRunTime utility. This makes it easy to see at a glance when jobs actually ran.

- Add formatRunTime() utility to convert ISO timestamps to readable format (e.g., "Mar 10, 2:32 PM")
- Replace getDurationMs() call with formatRunTime(detailRun.start) in the overview job list
- Job detail page and RunBars charts remain unchanged (duration still useful there)

feat(framebuffer): colorize Braille rendering with per-cell average RGB

Replace monochrome Braille rendering with colored output: compute the
average RGB of each 2×4 pixel block, convert to ANSI 256 color, and
wrap each braille glyph with the appropriate foreground color.

Dot placement is unchanged (luma threshold). Uses render_with callback
to color each cell independently.

commit.message:
feat(gate-lifecycle): add anti-pattern checkpoint tagging convention

Integrate anti-pattern registry into the full gate lifecycle through a
defined tag format convention ({AP-nn}) and skill updates to gate-plan,
gate-review, and gate-work. gate-template.md documents the tagging syntax.
gate-plan reads the registry during authoring and tags checkpoints.
gate-review validates tags during quality audits. gate-work reads the
registry during execution for verification rigor awareness.

Q10 gate deliverable.

commit.message:
Export SMITHR_SUBSTRATE so run-test.sh inherits it

Without export, run-test.sh (a child process) defaults to
"simulated" regardless of what maestro-sidecar.sh detected.
This caused iOS physical to hit the simulated code path.
